hypothesis: Mutating position 5 to aromatic residues (W/F/Y) will enhance SSTR2-specific interactions in the ECL2/TM5 pocket, while mutating position 11 to hydrophobic residues (L/I/V) will improve steric complementarity with SSTR2-unique TM6, thereby increasing the selectivity margin over off-targets.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current designs are achieving reasonable selectivity (Δmargin > 0), but structural quality remains a limiting factor. Tightening structural constraints and increasing candidate diversity are key to generating valid, selective SSTR2 ligands.

proposed_changes.rationale: Increasing the number of candidates improves the chances of generating structurally valid designs that pass QC gates.
Reducing the clash threshold will enforce better local geometry and reduce structural artifacts that lead to pLDDT=0.0 failures.
